mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If RSAFS shows consistent positive monthly growth but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of observations over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market, which typically precedes a slowdown in consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of such triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is low and housing starts (HOUST) are increasing, it indicates an accommodative monetary policy supporting a strong housing market, which boosts overall economic activity and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS is below 2.0% and HOUST growth exceeds 5% MoM, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: In periods of strong industrial production growth (INDPRO), an acceleration in manufacturing output signals robust corporate activity and future earnings potential, driving short-term positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5% MoM during a manufacturing expansion, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 30% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: A significant rise in inflation (CPIAUCSL) above a certain threshold erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, prompting the central bank to tighten monetary policy, which negatively impacts equity valuations and leads to lower S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL annual growth exceeds 3.0%, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered periods over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation, as indicated by positive monthly changes in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS), reflects a healthy and expanding economy, supporting consumer confidence and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows consistent positive monthly growth but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative over a 6-month period, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong GDP growth (GDPC1) and low unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a robust and efficient economy, leading to high corporate profitability and investor optimism, which drives sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 annual growth exceeds 2.5% and UNRATE is below 4.0%, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 35% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of aggressive monetary tightening, characterized by rapid Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) increases, the higher cost of capital and reduced liquidity negatively impact equity valuations, leading to short-term negative S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25 percentage points in a month during a tightening cycle, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered periods.
